front: Novel: Indian woman kills ship captain Half-Face; settles in Iowa with wheelchair-bound banker Bud Ripplemayer
Novel: protagonist's husband Prakash killed by radical terrorist Sukhwinder in a bombing while shopping for saris
Novel: protagonist changes names throughout (Jyoti, Jasmine, Jane, Jazzy)
Novel: Darrel Lutz hangs himself after his loan applications are repeatedly refused by Bud Ripplemayer
Short Story: inspired by Air India Flight 182 bombing by Sikh terrorists; narrator Shaila Bhave loses husband Vikram and two sons
Short Story: social worker Judith Templeton asks the narrator to help bereaved Indian-Canadian families sign government forms
Short Story: protagonist imagines her husband telling her to 'finish what we started together' in a Himalayan temple
Short Story: Panna Bhatt is offended by a production of David Mamet's Glengarry Glen Ross
Novel: retells The Scarlet Letter; Beigh Masters uses VR to travel to Mughal-era India; Hannah Easton falls in love with a prince
back: The Holder of the World (Bharati Mukherjee)

front: Novel: Calcutta native Tara writes her dissertation on Katherine Mansfield at Vassar and returns to India
